title: Browser Trust, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Headers · Contract Lens: Cookie Prefix Samesite
summary: A browser API reflects origins, combines credentials with wildcard sharing, treats CORS as access control, relies only on SameSite, and emits weak cookie and framing policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts cookie prefix samesite increment in browser request and response-sharing policy matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, lifetime, host-only or prefix constraints, partitioning decisions, rotation, and deletion consistently.
Protect cookie-authenticated mutations with safe-method semantics, tokens, trusted Origin or Referer, Fetch Metadata, and explicit exceptional-client handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Cookie Prefix Samesite · Theory Cookie Prefix Samesite
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8692 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep CORS response sharing distinct from CSRF mutation authorization; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, lifetime, host-only or prefix constraints, partitioning decisions, rotation, and deletion consistently.
